title: [PROJECT] Image Optimization & Performance
description: Convert gallery images to WebP format and implement Next.js Image component with modern loading strategies to reduce page weight from ~70MB to <10MB and improve LCP scores

title: Batch convert gallery PNGs to WebP using sharp CLI with quality optimization
description:
DEPENDENCIES: Install sharp-cli via `npm install -g sharp-cli` or use local sharp package.

FILES TO CREATE:
- `website/scripts/convert-to-webp.ts` - Node script for batch conversion
- `website/public/gallery/originals/` - Backup directory for original PNGs

FILES TO MODIFY:
- `website/src/lib/data.ts` - Update all `image` field values from `.png` to `.webp` extensions

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Create conversion script that:
   - Reads all PNG files from `website/public/gallery/*.png`
   - Converts each to WebP at 82% quality using sharp: `sharp(inputPath).webp({ quality: 82, effort: 6 }).toFile(outputPath)`
   - Moves originals to `website/public/gallery/originals/`
   - Logs file size reduction for each image
2. Run via `ts-node website/scripts/convert-to-webp.ts`
3. Update gallery data array in `data.ts`: find/replace all `/gallery/*.png` with `/gallery/*.webp`

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All 14 images in `website/public/gallery/` are WebP format
- Each WebP file is <500KB (target 200-400KB range)
- Original PNGs preserved in `website/public/gallery/originals/`
- `data.ts` has zero references to `.png` extensions in gallery image paths
- No 404 errors when viewing gallery page
- Total gallery directory size reduced from ~70MB to <6MB

title: Auto-generate base64 blur placeholders and integrate with gallery data schema
[PROJECT] SEO & Meta Tag Implementation
description: Implement comprehensive SEO foundation with Open Graph, Twitter Cards, structured data, and sitemap to improve social sharing and search visibility

title: Implement Next.js Metadata API for Open Graph and Twitter Cards
description: 1. Modify website/src/app/layout.tsx: Export default metadata object with { title: { template: '%s | Pawcasso Atelier', default: 'Pawcasso Atelier - Custom AI Pet Portraits' }, description: 'Transform your pet into stunning AI-generated art. Custom portraits for $9.', openGraph: { type: 'website', locale: 'en_US', url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app', siteName: 'Pawcasso Atelier', images: [{ url: '/gallery/[select-hero-image].png', width: 1200, height: 630, alt: 'Pawcasso Atelier Gallery' }] }, twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', title: 'Pawcasso Atelier - Custom AI Pet Portraits', description: 'Transform your pet into stunning AI-generated art for $9', images: ['/gallery/[select-hero-image].png'] } }. 2. Add to website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx: Export metadata with title: 'Gallery', description: 'Browse our collection of AI-generated pet portraits', openGraph override with images array from gallery. 3. Add to website/src/app/order/page.tsx: Export metadata with title: 'Order Custom Portrait', description: 'Order your custom AI pet portrait for $9', openGraph type: 'website'. Select best hero image from website/public/gallery/ directory. Test with curl -I https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app and validators.

title: Inject JSON-LD Structured Data (Product + ImageGallery Schemas)
description: 1. In website/src/app/order/page.tsx, add after opening div: <script type='application/ld+json' dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify({ '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'Product', name: 'Custom AI Pet Portrait', image: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/gallery/[hero-image].png', description: 'Transform your pet into stunning AI-generated artwork', brand: { '@type': 'Brand', name: 'Pawcasso Atelier' }, offers: { '@type': 'Offer', url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/order', priceCurrency: 'USD', price: '9.00', availability: 'https://schema.org/InStock' } }) }} />. 2. In website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx, import galleryImages from '@/lib/data' and add: <script type='application/ld+json' dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify({ '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'ImageGallery', name: 'Pawcasso Atelier Gallery', description: 'AI-generated pet portraits', image: galleryImages.map(img => ({ '@type': 'ImageObject', contentUrl: `https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app${img.src}`, name: img.title, description: img.caption })) }) }} />. Test with https://search.google.com/test/rich-results and https://validator.schema.org/.

title: Generate Dynamic Sitemap and Robots.txt with Next.js Route Handlers
description: 1. Create website/src/app/sitemap.ts: Export default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap { return [{ url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app', lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: 'weekly', priority: 1 }, { url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/gallery', lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: 'weekly', priority: 0.8 }, { url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/order', lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: 'monthly', priority: 1.0 }] }. Import MetadataRoute from 'next'. 2. Create website/src/app/robots.ts: Export default function robots(): MetadataRoute.Robots { return { rules: { userAgent: '*', allow: '/' }, sitemap: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/sitemap.xml' } }. 3. In website/src/app/layout.tsx metadata, add metadataBase: new URL('https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app'). Test with curl https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/sitemap.xml and curl https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/robots.txt after deployment.

title: [PROJECT] Order Form Pet Photo Upload
description: Enable customers to upload a photo of their pet during order submission, storing it securely with Vercel Blob for the artist to reference

title: Implement Vercel Blob API route for pet photo uploads
description:
**Files to create:**
- `website/src/app/api/upload/route.ts` - POST handler for image uploads

**Dependencies:**
```bash
cd website && npm install @vercel/blob
```

**Environment variables:**
- Add `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN=<token>` to `website/.env.local` (get from Vercel dashboard > Storage > Blob)
- Add same token to Vercel project settings (vercel.com project > Settings > Environment Variables)

**Implementation:**
- Export async POST handler: `export async function POST(request: NextRequest)`
- Parse multipart form data: `const formData = await request.formData(); const file = formData.get('file') as File`
- Validate: `if (!file || !['image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/webp'].includes(file.type)) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Invalid file type' }, { status: 400 })`
- Validate size: `if (file.size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'File too large' }, { status: 400 })`
- Upload: `const blob = await put(file.name, file, { access: 'public' })`
- Return: `NextResponse.json({ url: blob.url })`
- Add try/catch with 500 error response

**Acceptance criteria:**
- API returns 400 for invalid file types (test with .txt file)
- API returns 400 for files > 10MB (test with large file)
- API returns 200 with `{ url: 'https://...' }` for valid images
- Blob URL is publicly accessible (test in browser)
- Test with curl: `curl -F 'file=@test.jpg' http://localhost:3000/api/upload`

title: Integrate pet photo upload field into order form with Stripe metadata
description:
**Files to modify:** - `website/src/app/order/page.tsx` - Add file upload UI and logic **Implementation:** 1. Add state hooks: - `const [selectedFile, setSelectedFile] = useState(null)` - `const [previewUrl, setPreviewUrl] = useState(null)` - `const [uploadProgress, setUploadProgress] = useState(0)` - `const [uploadError, setUploadError] = useState(null)` 2. Add file input UI after email field: ```tsx
{previewUrl && } {uploadError &&

{uploadError}

}
``` 3. Implement `handleFileChange`: - Validate file size: `if (file.size > 10MB) setUploadError(...)` - Generate preview: `const url = URL.createObjectURL(file); setPreviewUrl(url)` - Clear previous errors 4. Modify existing form submit handler: - **Before** calling Stripe checkout API, if `selectedFile` exists: ```ts let petPhotoUrl = null; if (selectedFile) { setUploadProgress(50); const formData = new FormData(); formData.append('file', selectedFile); const res = await fetch('/api/upload', { method: 'POST', body: formData }); if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Photo upload failed'); const data = await res.json(); petPhotoUrl = data.url; setUploadProgress(100); } ``` - Pass `petPhotoUrl` to Stripe checkout API as metadata: `metadata: { petPhotoUrl: petPhotoUrl || '' }` 5. Add loading indicator: - Show progress bar when `uploadProgress > 0 && uploadProgress < 100` - Disable submit button during upload **Acceptance criteria:** - File input only accepts JPEG/PNG/WebP (other types grayed out) - Selecting 11MB file shows error message immediately - Valid image shows 32x32 preview thumbnail below input - Clicking submit with photo shows progress indicator - Stripe checkout session includes `metadata.petPhotoUrl` (verify in Stripe dashboard) - Submitting without photo works normally (no upload call) - Upload failure shows error message, doesn't break checkout flow

title: [PROJECT] Mobile UX & Visual Polish
description: Enhance mobile experience and perceived performance with loading states, responsive improvements, and smooth transitions

title: Implement gallery skeleton loading with Suspense and shimmer animation
description:
1. CREATE website/src/components/GallerySkeleton.tsx:
   - Export default component with shimmer animation using `animate-pulse bg-gradient-to-r from-gray-200 via-gray-300 to-gray-200 bg-[length:200%_100%]`
   - Render grid matching gallery: `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6`
   - Each skeleton card: aspect-ratio-square div + 2 text skeleton lines (w-3/4 and w-1/2)
   - Render 6 cards on mobile, 12 on desktop using conditional logic

2. MODIFY website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx:
   - Import React Suspense from 'react'
   - Extract existing gallery grid into new async component GalleryGrid (can be in same file)
   - Wrap <GalleryGrid /> with <Suspense fallback={<GallerySkeleton />}>
   - Keep filter buttons outside Suspense boundary

3. NO DEPENDENCIES needed (using built-in Next.js/React features)

4. ACCEPTANCE:
   - Run `npm run dev`, navigate to /gallery
   - Skeleton appears instantly (< 100ms)
   - Shimmer animation runs smoothly at 60fps
   - Grid layout matches actual gallery (same columns/gaps)
   - Transition from skeleton to real images is seamless (no layout shift)
   - Test in Chrome DevTools with "Slow 3G" throttling

title: Optimize gallery filters and order form for mobile touch interaction (320px-428px viewports)
description:
1. MODIFY website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx (filter buttons):
   - Wrap filter buttons container with: `overflow-x-auto scrollbar-hide snap-x snap-mandatory`
   - Individual buttons: add `snap-start flex-shrink-0` classes
   - Container: add `flex gap-2 pb-2 -mx-4 px-4` for edge-to-edge scroll with padding
   - Add `touch-action: pan-x` via className or inline style

2. MODIFY website/src/app/order/page.tsx (form inputs):
   - All input/textarea/select: add `w-full min-h-[44px] px-4 text-base` (44px = iOS recommended touch target)
   - Replace any `sm:` responsive width classes with full-width on mobile
   - Form container: add `space-y-4` (16px vertical spacing)
   - Labels: add `mb-2 text-sm font-medium` for better hierarchy
   - Submit button: ensure `min-h-[48px] w-full sm:w-auto` for prominent CTA

3. ADD TO website/src/app/globals.css:
   ```css
   @layer utilities {
     .scrollbar-hide::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }
     .scrollbar-hide { -ms-overflow-style: none; scrollbar-width: none; }
   }
   ```

4. ACCEPTANCE:
   - Chrome DevTools responsive mode: test 320px, 375px, 390px, 428px widths
   - Gallery filters scroll smoothly with finger swipe, snap to buttons
   - No horizontal overflow on order form at any tested width
   - All form inputs comfortably tappable (44x44px minimum)
   - Text inputs auto-zoom disabled on iOS (16px font-size minimum met)
   - Run Lighthouse mobile audit: accessibility score 95+
